docs.text: About bom-configuration is a simple, opinionated python configuration library. Usage This library is useful in the form of an application and library relationship. That is, your application consumes various libraries and a base configuration is built from the libraries. In each library, create a Config . # storage library from bom.configuration import Config specs = { "host": "localhost", "port": 5432, "password": "password", } config = Config.from_dict(specs) print(config.get("host")) # prints 'localhost' In the application, create an AppConfig . # application from bom.configuration import load, AppConfig specs = { "ENV": "prod", "app_name": "cool application", "app_version": "1.0.0", } config: AppConfig = load(specs) print(config.get("host")) # prints 'localhost' print(config.env) # prints 'prod'
docs.title: About

docs.text: bom-configuration is a simple, opinionated python configuration library.
docs.title: About

docs.text: This library is useful in the form of an application and library relationship. That is, your application consumes various libraries and a base configuration is built from the libraries. In each library, create a Config . # storage library from bom.configuration import Config specs = { "host": "localhost", "port": 5432, "password": "password", } config = Config.from_dict(specs) print(config.get("host")) # prints 'localhost' In the application, create an AppConfig . # application from bom.configuration import load, AppConfig specs = { "ENV": "prod", "app_name": "cool application", "app_version": "1.0.0", } config: AppConfig = load(specs) print(config.get("host")) # prints 'localhost' print(config.env) # prints 'prod'
docs.title: Usage

docs.text: Contributing To contribute, you must have pipenv and invoke installed. If you take a look at the tasks.py file, you will see various command. Everyone is welcome to open a PR. However, I recommend running invoke fmt test lint before pushing.
docs.title: Contributing
